_purpose: Per-PC baseline af installed dependencies + tooling. Bruges til cross-PC parity-check (PC1 ↔ PC2). Refresh efter hver dep-bump eller hver `npm ci`. Compare-script: scripts/check-install-parity.ps1 (foreslået forward-guard).
captured_via: manuel audit efter PR #579 ipKeyGenerator install-bug

parity_status.root: OK — all direct deps match lockfile
parity_status.backend: OK — all direct deps match lockfile
parity_status.frontend: OK — all direct deps match lockfile

remediation_history.2026-05-24T21:20+02:00: backend: npm install --prefix backend → express-rate-limit 7.5.1 → 8.5.2 (root cause of PR #579 test SyntaxError)
remediation_history.2026-05-24T21:30+02:00: root: npm install → first-ever install on PC2 (39 packages added: @supabase/* + dotenv + lint-staged)
remediation_history.2026-05-24T21:34+02:00: frontend: npm ci --prefix frontend → eslint/postcss/vite/supabase-js bumped to lockfile versions; `npm install` had falsely reported 'up to date' even though direct deps were 1-3 patches behind

known_lesson.npm_install_silently_skipped_frontend_up: On 2026-05-24 PC2, `npm install --prefix frontend` printed 'up to date' but left eslint/postcss/vite/@supabase/supabase-js 1-3 patches behind their lockfile-pinned versions. `npm ci --prefix frontend` fixed it. Recommendation: use `npm ci` instead of `npm install` for routine sync after `git pull`.
